info.name: Work-Flow-App API
info.description:
Complete API collection for Work-Flow-App Backend

**Base URL:** https://api.dev.workfloow.app (Dev Environment)

**Local Development:** http://localhost:8080

**Authentication:** JWT Bearer Token (obtained from /auth/login or /auth/signup)

**Available Roles:**
- COMPANY: Company owner/manager
- WORKER: Worker/employee
- ADMIN: System administrator

exec: // This script runs before every request
// You can add global pre-request logic here
// This script runs after every request
// You can add global test logic here

item.name: Authentication

exec: console.log("Access token saved to environment:", response.accessToken);
console.log("Refresh token saved to environment:", response.refreshToken);

request.description:
Register a new user account.

**Request Body:**
- username (string, required): 3-20 characters
- email (string, required): Valid email format
- password (string, required): Minimum 8 characters
- role (enum, required): COMPANY, WORKER, or ADMIN

**Response:**
- accessToken (string): JWT access token for API authentication
- refreshToken (string): Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens
- tokenType (string): Token type (always "Bearer")
- expiresIn (long): Access token expiration time in seconds (900 = 15 minutes)

**Token Lifetimes:**
- Access token: 15 minutes
- Refresh token: 30 days

**Note:** COMPANY role automatically creates a Company entity for the user.

exec: console.log("Access token saved to environment:", response.accessToken);
console.log("Refresh token saved to environment:", response.refreshToken);

request.description:
Authenticate user and receive JWT tokens.

**Request Body:**
- userName (string, required): Username
- password (string, required): Password

**Response:**
- accessToken (string): JWT access token for API authentication
- refreshToken (string): Refresh token for obtaining new access tokens
- tokenType (string): Token type (always "Bearer")
- expiresIn (long): Access token expiration time in seconds (900 = 15 minutes)

**Token Lifetimes:**
- Access token: 15 minutes
- Refresh token: 30 days

**Multi-Device Support:** Max 5 active sessions per user. Oldest session auto-revoked when limit exceeded.

Refresh access token using refresh token.

**Request Body:**
- refreshToken (string, required): Valid refresh token from login/signup

**Response:**
- accessToken (string): New JWT access token
- refreshToken (string): New refresh token (token rotation)
- tokenType (string): Token type (always "Bearer")
- expiresIn (long): Access token expiration time in seconds

**Token Rotation:** Old refresh token is automatically revoked and a new one is issued for security.

**Use Case:** When access token expires (after 15 minutes), use this endpoint to get a new one without re-authenticating.

Logout from current device by revoking refresh token.

**Request Body:**
- refreshToken (string, required): Refresh token to revoke

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Security Note:** Access token remains valid until expiration (15 minutes). For immediate invalidation, implement token blacklisting.

**Use Case:** User logs out from current device/browser.

Logout from all devices by revoking all refresh tokens for the authenticated user.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Security Feature:** Invalidates all active sessions across all devices (up to 5 sessions per user).

**Use Cases:**
- Security breach response
- Password change
- User wants to logout from all devices remotely

Request a password reset verification code to be sent to the provided email.

**Request Body:**
- email (string, required): Valid email format

**Response:**
- message (string): Generic success message (returned even if email doesn't exist for security)

**Security Features:**
- Does not reveal if email exists in the system
- Verification code expires after 60 minutes
- Maximum 3 active reset codes per user
- Oldest code auto-deleted when limit exceeded
- Codes are single-use only
- 6-digit numeric code (100000-999999)

**Email Content:**
- 6-digit verification code displayed prominently
- Professional email template with gradient styling
- Email includes expiry warning (60 minutes)
- Security notice about unauthorized requests

**Use Case:** User forgot their password and needs to reset it

response.name: Forgot Password - Success

request.description:
Reset password using the verification code received via email.

**Request Body:**
- email (string, required): Valid email format - the email that requested the reset
- code (string, required): 6-digit verification code from email (e.g., "583921")
- newPassword (string, required): Minimum 8 characters

**Response:**
- message (string): Success message

**Security Features:**
- Validates email + code combination (code must belong to the email)
- Validates code exists, not expired, and not used
- Marks code as used after successful reset
- Password is BCrypt encoded before storage
- Automatically revokes ALL refresh tokens (forces re-login on all devices)
- Code can only be used once
- Code expires after 60 minutes

**Validation:**
- Code format: Must be exactly 6 digits (pattern: ^\\d{6}$)
- Email format: Must be valid email

**Error Cases:**
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid, expired, or already used code
- 400 Bad Request: Code does not match email
- 400 Bad Request: Password too short (< 8 characters)
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid email or code format

**Post-Reset Actions:**
- User must login again with new password
- All active sessions are invalidated

**Use Case:** User enters the 6-digit code from email along with their email and new password

response.name: Reset Password - Success

exec: // Store JWT and Refresh tokens for future requests

request.description:
Complete worker signup using an invitation token. Creates worker account with associated company.

**Request Body:**
- invitationToken (string, required): UUID invitation token from email
- email (string, required): Worker email (must match invitation email)
- name (string, required): Worker's full name (2-100 characters)
- username (string, required): Desired username (3-20 characters)
- password (string, required): Password (minimum 8 characters)

**Response:**
- workerId (long): Created worker ID
- name (string): Worker's name
- email (string): Worker's email
- username (string): Worker's username
- companyName (string): Associated company name
- message (string): Success message

**Business Logic:**
- Validates invitation token (must be valid, not expired, not already used)
- Token expires after 7 days from invitation creation
- Email in request must match invitation email
- Creates User account with WORKER role
- Creates Worker entity with name and company association
- Marks invitation as used
- Username must be unique across the system
- User UUID is auto-generated

**Security Features:**
- Token-based verification ensures worker is invited
- Email verification prevents token hijacking
- Password is BCrypt encoded before storage
- Token can only be used once
- Invitation status updated to ACCEPTED on success

**Status Code:** 200 OK

**Error Responses:**
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid or expired invitation token
- 400 Bad Request: Invitation already used
- 400 Bad Request: Email does not match invitation
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid validation (name, username, password format)
- 409 Conflict: Username already exists
- 404 Not Found: Company not found or archived

**Use Case:** Worker receives invitation email with token, creates account using this endpoint

item.description:
Authentication endpoints for user registration, login, token refresh, and logout.

**Token Management:**
- Access tokens expire after 15 minutes
- Refresh tokens expire after 30 days
- Token rotation implemented for security (refresh tokens are single-use)
- Max 5 active sessions per user
- Device tracking (User-Agent + IP address)

**Security Features:**
- Automatic revocation of oldest session when limit exceeded
- Single-device logout via /logout
- All-device logout via /logout-all
- Daily cleanup of expired tokens

item.name: Company

request.description:
Retrieve the authenticated company's profile.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Response:**
- id (long): Company ID
- name (string): Company name
- email (string): Company email
- telephone (string): Phone number
- mobile (string): Mobile number
- fax (string): Fax number
- addressLine1, addressLine2, addressLine3 (string): Address details
- town (string): Town/city
- country (string): Country
- postcode (string): Postal code
- contactEmail (string): Contact email
- contactNumber (string): Contact number
- archived (boolean): Archive status
- createdAt (datetime): Creation timestamp
- updatedAt (datetime): Last update timestamp

response.name: Get Company Profile - Success
response.body:
{
    "id": 1,
    "name": "Tech Solutions Inc",
    "email": "company1@example.com",
    "telephone": "1234567890",
    "mobile": "0987654321",

    "town": "New York",

    "updatedAt": "2025-10-23T10:00:00"
}

request.description:
Update the authenticated company's profile.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): 2-150 characters
- addressLine1 (string, optional): Max 255 characters
- addressLine2 (string, optional): Max 255 characters
- addressLine3 (string, optional): Max 255 characters
- town (string, optional): Max 100 characters
- country (string, optional): Max 100 characters
- postcode (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- telephone (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- fax (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- email (string, optional): Valid email format, max 100 characters
- contactEmail (string, optional): Valid email format, max 100 characters
- contactNumber (string, optional): Max 50 characters

**Business Rules:**
- Company name must be unique (case-insensitive)
- Updating to the same name (different case) is allowed

**Response:** Updated company profile (same format as GET)

request.description:
Retrieve company dashboard with statistics.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Response:**
- companyId (long): Company ID
- companyName (string): Company name
- totalWorkers (long): Total number of workers (active + archived)
- totalClients (long): Total number of clients
- activeWorkers (long): Number of active (non-archived) workers
- archivedWorkers (long): Number of archived workers

item.description: Company profile and dashboard management endpoints.

item.name: Workers

request.description:
Create a new worker with user account.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): 2-100 characters
- initials (string, optional): Max 10 characters
- telephone (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- email (string, optional): Valid email format, max 100 characters
- username (string, required): 3-20 characters (for worker login)
- password (string, required): Minimum 8 characters

**Business Logic:**
- Creates both Worker entity and User account with ROLE_WORKER
- Worker is associated with the company of the authenticated user
- Username must be unique across all users
- Email must be unique if provided

**Response:**
- id (long): Worker ID
- name (string): Worker name
- initials (string): Initials
- telephone (string): Telephone number
- mobile (string): Mobile number
- email (string): Email address
- username (string): Login username
- loginLocked (boolean): Account lock status
- archived (boolean): Archive status
- createdAt (datetime): Creation timestamp
- updatedAt (datetime): Last update timestamp

Retrieve all workers for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Response:** Array of worker objects (same format as Create Worker response)

**Note:** Returns all workers (including archived) belonging to the company.

response.name: Get All Workers - Success

]

request.description:
Retrieve a specific worker by ID.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Worker ID

**Response:** Worker object (same format as Create Worker response)

**Authorization:** Only workers belonging to the authenticated company can be accessed.

Full replacement update of an existing worker's details.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Worker ID

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): 2-100 characters
- initials (string, optional): Max 10 characters
- telephone (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- email (string, required): Valid email format, max 100 characters

**REST Semantics:** PUT performs full resource replacement. All fields are sent (required fields must be provided).

**Note:** 
- Username and password cannot be updated through this endpoint
- Email is synced with the associated User entity

**Response:** Updated worker object (same format as Create Worker response)

**Authorization:** Only workers belonging to the authenticated company can be updated.

Partial update of an existing worker's details.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Worker ID

**Request Body (all fields optional - only send fields to update):**
- name (string): 2-100 characters
- initials (string): Max 10 characters
- telephone (string): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string): Max 20 characters
- email (string): Valid email format, max 100 characters

**REST Semantics:** PATCH performs partial update. Only non-null fields in the request body are updated.

**Note:** 
- Username and password cannot be updated through this endpoint
- If email is updated, it is synced with the associated User entity

**Response:** Updated worker object (same format as Create Worker response)

**Authorization:** Only workers belonging to the authenticated company can be updated.

Soft delete a worker (archive).

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Worker ID

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Business Logic:**
- Sets worker.archived = true
- Worker remains in database but is marked as archived
- Archived workers still appear in getAllWorkers() but are counted separately in dashboard

**Authorization:** Only workers belonging to the authenticated company can be deleted.

Invite a new worker by email. Creates an invitation record and sends an email with a unique verification token.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- email (string, required): Worker's email address
- firstName (string, required): Worker's first name
- lastName (string, required): Worker's last name

**Response:**
- id (long): Invitation ID
- companyId (long): Company ID
- email (string): Worker's email
- firstName (string): Worker's first name
- lastName (string): Worker's last name
- status (string): Invitation status (PENDING)
- token (string): Unique verification token (for testing only, not sent in production)
- expiresAt (datetime): Token expiration timestamp (7 days from creation)
- createdAt (datetime): Invitation creation timestamp

**Business Logic:**
- Generates unique verification token valid for 7 days
- Sends invitation email with signup link containing token
- Token can be used for worker signup
- Only one pending invitation per email allowed

**Status Code:** 201 Created

**Error Responses:**
- 400 Bad Request: Invalid email or missing required fields
- 409 Conflict: Active invitation already exists for this email

**Post-request Script:** Automatically sets {{invitation_token}} variable on success

response.name: Invite Worker by Email - Conflict
response.body:
{
    "timestamp": "2025-11-14T10:30:00",
    "status": 409,
    "error": "Conflict",
    "message": "Active invitation already exists for this email",
    "path": "/api/v1/workers/invite"
}

request.description:
Get all worker invitations for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Response:** Array of invitation objects
- id (long): Invitation ID
- companyId (long): Company ID
- email (string): Worker's email
- firstName (string): Worker's first name
- lastName (string): Worker's last name
- status (string): Invitation status (PENDING, ACCEPTED, EXPIRED, CANCELLED)
- expiresAt (datetime): Token expiration timestamp
- createdAt (datetime): Invitation creation timestamp
- acceptedAt (datetime, optional): When invitation was accepted

**Business Logic:**
- Returns all invitations for the authenticated company
- Invitations are ordered by creation date (newest first)
- Expired invitations are automatically marked as EXPIRED

**Authorization:** Only invitations belonging to the authenticated company are returned.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

Check invitation token validity and retrieve invitation details.

**Authentication:** NOT REQUIRED (Public endpoint)

**Path Parameter:**
- token (string): Invitation token from email URL

**Response:**
- valid (boolean): Whether the invitation is still valid (not used, not expired)
- email (string): Email address of the invitee
- companyName (string): Name of the company sending the invitation
- status (string): PENDING, ACCEPTED, or EXPIRED
- expiresAt (datetime): Expiration timestamp

**Use Case:**
- Frontend calls this endpoint when user clicks invitation link
- Pre-fills and disables email field on signup form
- Shows company name and validates token before user fills form

**Status Code:** 200 OK

item.description: Worker management endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting workers.

request.description:
Create a new client for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): 2-150 characters
- email (string, optional): Valid email format
- telephone (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- address (string, optional): Full address details

**Business Logic:**
- Client is associated with the company of the authenticated user
- Name should be unique per company (enforced at service/repository level)

**Response:** Created client object with metadata (id, timestamps, archived flag).

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

item.name: Customers
item.description: Customer management endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and deleting customers.

request.description:
Create a new customer for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): 2-150 characters
- email (string, optional): Valid email format
- telephone (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- mobile (string, optional): Max 20 characters
- address (object, optional): Nested address details
  - houseNumber (string, optional): House or building number
  - street (string, optional): Street name
  - city (string, optional): City name
  - county (string, optional): County or region
  - postalCode (string, optional): UK postal code
  - country (string, optional): Country name

**Business Logic:**
- Customer is associated with the company of the authenticated user
- Name should be unique per company (enforced at service/repository level)

**Response:** Created customer object with metadata (id, timestamps, archived flag).

Retrieve all customers for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Response:** Array of customer objects (same format as Create Customer response)

**Note:** Returns all customers (including archived) belonging to the company.

Retrieve a specific customer by ID.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Customer ID

**Response:** Customer object (same format as Create Customer response)

**Authorization:** Only customers belonging to the authenticated company can be accessed.

body.raw:
{
    "name": "GlobalTech Solutions Ltd.",
    "email": "support@globaltech.com",
    "telephone": "555555555",
    "mobile": "999999999",

        "street": "Innovation Drive",
        "city": "London",
        "county": "Greater London",
        "postalCode": "EC2A 2BB",
        "country": "United Kingdom"
    },
    "archived": false
}

  - postalCode (string, optional): UK postal code

**Response:** Updated customer object

**Authorization:** Only customers belonging to the authenticated company can be updated.

request.description:
Delete a customer by ID.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Customer ID

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Authorization:** Only customers belonging to the authenticated company can be deleted.

item.name: Job Templates
item.description: Job Template management endpoints for creating templates, managing template fields, and organizing dynamic job workflows. Templates are the blueprint for Job instances.

request.description:
Create a new job template for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- name (string, required): Template name (2-150 characters, unique per company)
- description (string, optional): Template description
- isDefault (boolean, optional): Set as default template for the company (default: false). Only one template can be default per company.

**Business Logic:**
- Template is associated with the company of the authenticated user.
- If isDefault is true, any existing default template will be unmarked as default.

**Response:** Created template object with metadata (id, timestamps, archived flag, isDefault).

response.name: Create Job Template - Success

{
    "id": 3,
    "companyId": 1,
    "name": "Maintenance Job",
    "description": "Standard maintenance workflow template for equipment servicing",
    "archived": false,
    "isDefault": false,
    "createdAt": "2025-11-14T10:00:00",
    "updatedAt": "2025-11-14T10:00:00"
}

request.description:
Update an existing job template.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Template ID

**Request Body:**

**Authorization:** Only templates belonging to the authenticated company can be updated.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

request.description:
Deletes a job template and all associated fields.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Template ID

**Business Logic:**
- Performs a permanent delete (hard delete) of the template and its fields.

**Authorization:** Only templates belonging to the authenticated company can be deleted.

**Status Code:** 204 No Content

Add a field to a job template.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Request Body:**
- templateId (long, required): ID of the parent template
- name (string, required): Internal field key (unique per template)
- label (string, required): User-facing label
- jobFieldType (enum, required): Field type (TEXT, NUMBER, CHECKBOX, DROPDOWN, etc.)
- required (boolean, required): Whether the field is mandatory for job creation/completion
- options (string, optional): JSON string for dropdown/radio options (e.g., "[\"Option A\", \"Option B\"]")
- orderIndex (int, required): Order in which the field should appear

**Authorization:** Parent template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 201 Created

Retrieve all fields for a specific job template.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Template ID

**Response:** Array of job template field objects.

**Authorization:** Parent template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

Retrieve a single field by its ID.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Field ID

**Authorization:** Parent template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

Update a template field.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Field ID

**Request Body:** (All fields optional, provide only those to update)
- label (string, optional): User-facing label
- jobFieldType (enum, optional): Field type
- required (boolean, optional): Mandatory status
- options (string, optional): JSON string for dropdown/radio options
- orderIndex (int, optional): Order index

**Note:** `templateId` and `name` (internal key) cannot be updated via this endpoint.

**Authorization:** Field's parent template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

Delete a job template field.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Field ID

**Business Logic:**
- Performs a permanent delete (hard delete) of the field.

**Authorization:** Field's parent template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 204 No Content

Retrieve a job template along with all of its fields in a nested structure.

**Authentication:** Required (COMPANY role)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Template ID

**Response:** Job template object including a nested list of field objects.

**Authorization:** Template must belong to the authenticated company.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

response.name: Get Template With Fields - Success

item.name: Jobs
item.description: Job management endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and managing job instances based on job templates.

request.description:
Create a new job instance based on a job template.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Request Body:**
- templateId (long, required): The job template ID to use
- customerId (long, required): The customer associated with this job
- clientId (long, optional): The client associated with this job
- assignedWorkerId (long, optional): The worker assigned to this job
- status (string, required): Job status (e.g., NEW, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, ON_HOLD, CANCELLED)
- fieldValues (object, required): Map of field ID to value (e.g., {"101": "John Doe", "102": "Medium"})
- assetIds (array of long, optional): IDs of assets to assign to this job

**Business Logic:**
- Job is associated with the company of the authenticated user
- Must reference a valid template ID
- Customer must belong to the same company (required field)
- Client and worker must belong to the same company
- All required template fields must have values in fieldValues
- Assets must be available, not archived, and belong to the same company
- Asset assignment will mark assets as unavailable

**Response:** Created job object with id, companyId, templateId, customerId, clientId, assignedWorkerId, status, archived flag, timestamps, fieldValues, and assetIds

**Status Code:** 201 Created

Retrieve all jobs for the authenticated company.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Response:** Array of job objects

**Note:** Returns all jobs (including archived) belonging to the company.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

response.name: Get All Jobs - Success

request.description:
Retrieve a specific job by ID.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Job ID

**Response:** Job object (same format as Create Job response)

**Authorization:** Only jobs belonging to the authenticated company can be accessed.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

Update an existing job.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Job ID

**Request Body:**
- customerId (long, optional): The customer associated with this job
- clientId (long, optional): The client associated with this job
- assignedWorkerId (long, optional): The worker assigned to this job
- status (string, optional): Job status (NEW, IN_PROGRESS, COMPLETED, ON_HOLD, CANCELLED)
- archived (boolean, optional): Archive status
- fieldValues (object, optional): Map of field ID to updated values
- workflowId (long, optional): ID of the workflow to assign to this job

**Business Logic:**
- Only jobs belonging to the authenticated company can be updated
- Customer must belong to the same company
- Client and worker must belong to the same company
- Cannot change the template after creation
- workflowId can only be set if the job currently has no workflow assigned. Once a workflow is assigned to a job it cannot be changed or replaced. Sending a workflowId on a job that already has a workflow returns 400 Bad Request with the message "Workflow cannot be changed once assigned to a job"
- Omitting workflowId (or sending it as null) leaves the existing workflow assignment untouched

**Response:** Updated job object

**Status Code:** 200 OK

response.name: Update Job - Error: Workflow Already Assigned (400)

request.description:
Soft delete (archive) a job by ID.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Path Parameter:**
- id (long): Job ID

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Business Logic:**
- Sets job.archived = true
- Job remains in database but is marked as archived
- Archived jobs still appear in getAllJobs() but are marked as archived

**Authorization:** Only jobs belonging to the authenticated company can be deleted.

**Status Code:** 204 No Content

Retrieve all jobs for a specific job template.

**Authentication:** Required (Bearer token in Authorization header)

**Path Parameter:**
- templateId (long): Job Template ID

**Response:** Array of job objects (same format as Get All Jobs response)

**Note:** Returns all jobs (including archived) belonging to the authenticated company that were created from the specified template.

**Authorization:** Only jobs belonging to the authenticated company can be accessed.

**Status Code:** 200 OK

response.name: Get Jobs by Template - Success

item.name: Assets
item.description: Asset management endpoints for creating, reading, updating, and managing company assets with depreciation tracking.

request.description:
Create a new company asset

```bash
curl -X POST "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{
    "name": "Company Van A1",
    "assetTag": "VAN-001",
    "serialNumber": "SN-2025-AX11",
    "purchasePrice": 30000.00,
    "purchaseDate": "2023-01-01",
    "depreciationRate": 20.00,
    "salvageValue": 5000.00
  }'
```

Fetch asset details

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets/10" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

Retrieve paginated list of assets

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets?page=0&size=20&available=true&archived=false" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

Update non-immutable fields of an asset

```bash
curl -X PUT "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets/10" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "name": "Company Van A1 (Updated)" }'
```

Archive (soft delete) an asset

```bash
curl -X POST "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets/10/archive" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

Get depreciation-based current value

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets/10/value?asOfDate=2025-11-24" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

Get asset statistics for the company

**Response:**
- totalAssets (long): Total number of assets
- activeAssets (long): Number of active (non-archived) assets
- archivedAssets (long): Number of archived assets
- totalValue (BigDecimal): Total purchase value of all assets
- currentValue (BigDecimal): Current depreciated value of all assets
- totalDepreciation (BigDecimal): Total depreciation amount

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/assets/statistics" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

item.name: Asset Assignments
item.description: Asset assignment endpoints for assigning assets to jobs, returning assets, and tracking assignment history.

request.description:
Assign an asset to a job (sets asset.available=false)

```bash
curl -X POST "{{base_url}}/api/v1/asset-assignments/assign" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "assetId": 10, "jobId": 3, "notes": "Used for transport to client site" }'
```

Return an asset from a job (sets returnedAt and asset.available=true)

```bash
curl -X POST "{{base_url}}/api/v1/asset-assignments/return" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}" \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{ "assetId": 10, "jobId": 3, "notes": "Returned after completion" }'
```

Get the assignment history for an asset (latest first)

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/asset-assignments/asset/10/history" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

response.name: Asset Assignment History - Success
response.body:
[
    {
        "assignmentId": 200,
        "jobId": 3,
        "jobTitle": "Office Installation",
        "assignedAt": "2025-11-21T09:00:00",
        "returnedAt": "2025-11-23T17:00:00",
        "durationDays": 2,
        "notes": "Used for transport to client site",
        "status": "COMPLETED"
    }
]

query.description: If true, returns only active assignments. If false, returns all assignments including returned assets.

request.description:
Get assets assigned to a specific job

**Query Parameters:**
- onlyActive (boolean, optional): Filter for active assignments only (default: false)
  - true: Only currently assigned assets
  - false: All assignments including historical/returned assets

```bash
curl -X GET "{{base_url}}/api/v1/asset-assignments/job/3?onlyActive=true" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer {{jwt_token}}"
```

item.name: Job Workflow Step Visit Logs

request.description:
Add a new visit log to a specific workflow step.

**Authentication:** Required

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): The ID of the workflow step

**Request Body:**
- visitDate (string, required): Format YYYY-MM-DD
- timeIn (string, required): Format HH:MM:SS
- timeOut (string, optional): Format HH:MM:SS
- description (string, optional): Visit details

**Business Logic:**
- Validates that the step belongs to the user's company
- Logs an activity record (VISIT_LOGGED) in the timeline
- Associates the visit log with the currently authenticated user

**Response:**
- id (long): Visit log ID
- visitDate (string): Visit date
- timeIn (string): Time in
- timeOut (string): Time out
- description (string): Description
- loggedById (long): ID of the user who logged it
- createdAt (datetime): Creation timestamp
- updatedAt (datetime): Last update timestamp

Update an existing visit log.

**Authentication:** Required

**Path Parameter:**
- visitLogId (long): The ID of the visit log

**Request Body (all optional):**
- visitDate (string): Format YYYY-MM-DD
- timeIn (string): Format HH:MM:SS
- timeOut (string): Format HH:MM:SS
- description (string): Visit details

**Business Logic:**
- User can only update their own visit logs (checked via loggedById)
- Logs an activity record (VISIT_UPDATED) in the timeline

**Response:** Updated visit log object

Delete a visit log.

**Authentication:** Required

**Path Parameter:**
- visitLogId (long): The ID of the visit log

**Response:** 204 No Content

**Business Logic:**
- User can only delete their own visit logs (checked via loggedById)
- Logs an activity record (VISIT_DELETED) in the timeline

Retrieve all visit logs for a specific workflow step.

**Authentication:** Required

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): The ID of the workflow step

**Response:** Array of visit log objects, ordered by visitDate (descending) and timeIn (descending).

**Business Logic:**
- Validates that the workflow step belongs to the user's company

response.name: Get Visit Logs - Success

**Authorization:** The worker must be assigned to at least one step in this workflow.

Get details of a specific step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to this specific step.

Change step status from NOT_STARTED to STARTED.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to the step.

Change step status from STARTED to COMPLETED.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to the step.

Get all comments for a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to this step.

Get all attachments for a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to this step.

Get discussion timeline (Comments & Attachments) for a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

Get all visit logs for a specific step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

**Authorization:** Worker must be assigned to this step.

Add a comment to a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Request Body:**
- content (string, required): The comment text
- type (string, required): The discussion type (e.g., GENERAL, ISSUE)

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

Upload an attachment to a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Body (form-data):**
- file (file, required): The file to upload
- type (string, required): The discussion type (e.g., GENERAL, ISSUE)
- description (string, optional): Description of the attachment

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

Add a new visit log to a step.

**Authentication:** Required (WORKER role)

**Request Body:**
- visitDate (string, required): Date of the visit (YYYY-MM-DD)
- timeIn (string, required): Start time (HH:MM:SS)
- timeOut (string, optional): End time (HH:MM:SS)
- description (string, optional): Details of the visit

**Path Parameter:**
- stepId (long): ID of the step

item.description: Endpoints for workers to manage their assigned workflows and steps.
